recipeEngine.description: Master recipe database. Creators can use these as templates or pull individual ingredients/utensils into their custom games.

globalInventory.description: All unique ingredients across all recipes. Creators can browse and add any of these to their custom game.

globalUtensils.description: All unique utensils across all recipes.

recipes.name: Efo Egusi Soup
recipes.description: Rich melon seed soup with leafy greens. The dish that started this game.
recipes.alternateTitle: Who Put Tomatoes In My Egusi?

bases_grains.onSelect: Congratulations. You are officially making Egusi. The bar is on the floor.
bases_grains.onSkip: Without egusi, this is just... stew with identity issues. Pick the egusi.

proteins.onSelect: Solid choice. The goat has given its consent... retroactively.
The default Nigerian party meat. Nobody will complain. Suspicious.
Ah! The smell that announces 'something serious is cooking.'
Oga! We see you! This is not just cooking, this is a flex.

vegetables.onSelect: The people's champion. Safe. Respected. Expected.
Diaspora detected. Your aunty in Ibadan just felt a disturbance.
You've chosen pain. Literally. Hope you washed it 47 times.

oils_fats.onSelect: Red gold. Your pot will now have that legitimate Nigerian glow.
Groundnut oil for egusi? You like to live dangerously.

seasonings.onSelect: The foundation of flavor. Grandma approves this specific choice.
seasonings.onSkip: Interesting decision. Very interesting. The ancestors are... confused.

seasonings.onSelect: Fresh pepper. The foundation of heat. Your pot will have DEPTH.
Crayfish! The umami of the gods! Your soup will have DEPTH.
Iru! The secret weapon. You know the real recipe.
Seasoning cubes. Grandma will side-eye but the flavor doesn't lie.
Salt. The quiet MVP of every dish.

wildcards.onSelect: Ah. One of THOSE cooks. The group chat has been notified.
If you add carrots, we won't stop you. But we will screenshot this.
Experimental chef detected. Even the mushrooms are confused.

heatSources.name: Charcoal Stove
heatSources.onSelect: Smoky flavor incoming. Your clothes will smell like commitment.

optional.name: Sieve (for Egusi)
optional.onSelect: Perfect, fluffy egusi grains await. This is pro-level.

recipes.name: Jollof Rice
recipes.description: The legendary party rice. Your method will either unite or divide nations.
recipes.alternateTitle: Ghana Started It, Nigeria Perfected It

bases_grains.onSelect: Long grain parboiled rice. The only acceptable choice. Fight me.
bases_grains.onSkip: No rice? Are you making jollof stew or jollof... nothing?

proteins.onSelect: Chicken and jollof. Name a more iconic duo. I'll wait.
Goat meat jollof? You're cooking for owambe!
Turkey in jollof? Diaspora Christmas energy. We respect it.

vegetables.onSelect: Fresh tomatoes. Non-negotiable. This is the red in your red rice.
Tatashe! The pepper that gives jollof its signature redness.
Fresh scotch bonnet. Heat that builds character.
Onions. The base of the base of the base.

oils_fats.onSelect: Vegetable oil. Neutral, reliable, won't steal the show.
Butter in jollof? You've been watching those fusion chefs on YouTube.

seasonings.onSelect: Tomato paste. Concentrated umami. The depth charge.
Maggi cubes. Don't let anyone shame you. Flavor is flavor.
Thyme. The herb that whispers 'party rice.'
Curry powder. Golden color. Golden flavor. Golden everything.
Bay leaves. The silent flavor ninjas of jollof.
Salt. Because unsalted jollof is a war crime.
Garlic. Because we're not animals.
Ginger. The warmth that makes people ask 'what's your secret?'

wildcards.onSelect: Coconut jollof? You've started an international incident. Ghana is watching.
Green peas for color. Your presentation will be questioned but not convicted.

heatSources.name: Charcoal Stove
heatSources.onSelect: Party jollof on firewood? You understand the assignment!

optional.name: Sieve (Wash Rice)
optional.onSelect: Washed rice is happy rice. No starch drama.

recipes.name: Amala & Ewedu with Gbegiri
recipes.description: Dark yam flour swallow with jute leaves soup and bean soup. The Ibadan special.
recipes.alternateTitle: Are You Even Yoruba If You Haven't Tried This?

bases_grains.onSelect: Yam flour for amala. Dark, smooth, and deeply traditional.

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat in your gbegiri. The Ibadan way.
Ponmo! Chewy, delicious, and essential for the full experience.

vegetables.onSelect: Water leaf for ewedu? Some do it. You're bold.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil in your gbegiri. The red that makes it right.

seasonings.onSelect: Iru! Without it, your ewedu is just... green water. Sorry.
Crayfish in ewedu. Flavor that whispers rather than shouts.
Pepper. Because bland ewedu is a personal attack on Yoruba culture.
Onions for the gbegiri base. Foundation flavor.
Salt. Even the most traditional food needs it.

grindingDebate.question: Team Turning Stick or Team Whisk?

optional.name: Sieve (Smooth Ewedu)
optional.onSelect: Smooth ewedu is a flex. Your arms will hurt but your soul will smile.

recipes.name: Suya (Spiced Grilled Meat)
recipes.description: Spicy, peanut-crusted grilled meat on skewers. Street food royalty.
recipes.alternateTitle: Mallam's Secret Spice Mix

proteins.onSelect: Beef suya. The classic. The standard. The reason people go out at 9pm.
Chicken suya. For those who want the flavor without the chew.
Turkey suya? Diaspora detected. We approve.

vegetables.onSelect: Raw sliced onions on the side. Non-negotiable. The crunch is part of the experience.
Fresh tomatoes on the side. Balance for the heat.

oils_fats.onSelect: Groundnut oil for brushing. The peanut theme continues.

seasonings.onSelect: Yaji! The secret blend. Ground peanut, cayenne, ginger, garlic, and mystery. This IS suya.
Salt. Even Mallam uses salt. Don't overthink this.
Fresh ginger in the marinade. The warmth behind the fire.
Garlic. Because meat without garlic is just... sadness.

wildcards.onSelect: Honey glaze suya? Sweet and spicy. Mallam is raising an eyebrow but he's intrigued.

heatSources.name: Charcoal Grill
heatSources.onSelect: Charcoal grill. The smoke, the flame, the Mallam energy. This is it.

heatSources.name: Oven Broiler
heatSources.onSelect: Oven suya? It works. It's just... not the same. You know it. I know it.

optional.name: Cast Iron Pan
optional.onSelect: Pan-seared suya. For when you can't grill outside. Respect the hustle.

recipes.name: Akara (Bean Cakes)
recipes.description: Deep-fried bean cakes. Crispy outside, fluffy inside. Breakfast of champions.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Sound of Morning in Lagos

bases_grains.onSelect: Black-eyed peas. Peeled by hand or by machine. The soul of akara.

proteins.onSelect: Eggs in akara batter? Some swear by it. Others will fight you. Choose your fighter.

vegetables.onSelect: Onions in akara. The crunch. The sweetness. Non-negotiable.
Fresh pepper. Akara without pepper is just... fried bean sadness.

oils_fats.onSelect: Vegetable oil, deep and hot. The bath that transforms beans into gold.
Palm oil akara? Old school. Your grandmother just smiled somewhere.

seasonings.onSelect: Salt. The difference between akara and bean-flavored cardboard.
A little Maggi in the batter? Your secret is safe with us.
Crayfish in akara? Coastal influence. Adds depth.

grindingDebate.question: Team Blender or Team Grinding Stone?

heatSources.name: Charcoal Stove
heatSources.onSelect: Firewood akara. The authentic street vendor taste.

recipes.name: Moi Moi (Steamed Bean Pudding)
recipes.description: Steamed savory bean pudding. Party staple. Jollof's silent partner.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Unsung Hero of Every Nigerian Party

bases_grains.onSelect: Peeled beans. The canvas for your moi moi masterpiece.

proteins.onSelect: Whole boiled egg in the center. The treasure at the heart of moi moi.
Corned beef moi moi. Rich, savory, and deeply satisfying.
Fish in moi moi. Coastal elegance.
Prawn moi moi? Oga! This is not just breakfast, this is a statement.

vegetables.onSelect: Onions blended into the batter. Flavor you can't see but can't miss.
Tatashe! The pepper that gives moi moi its beautiful color.
Scotch bonnet. Gentle heat that builds.

oils_fats.onSelect: Oil in the batter. The difference between moi moi and bean brick.
Palm oil moi moi. Redder. Richer. More traditional.

seasonings.onSelect: Crayfish. The umami background vocal that makes the song.
Maggi. Flavor shortcut. No judgment here.
Salt. Under-salted moi moi is a tragedy in a leaf.

wildcards.onSelect: Butter in moi moi? Rich. Creamy. Your aunty will ask for the recipe. Secretly.

grindingDebate.question: Team Blender or Team Grinding Stone?

recipes.name: Pounded Yam & Egusi Soup
recipes.description: Smooth, stretchy yam swallow with rich egusi soup. The heavyweight champion combo.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Sunday Special That Tests Your Stamina

bases_grains.onSelect: Fresh yam for pounding. You've chosen... effort. Respect.
Egusi for the soup. The perfect partner to pounded yam.

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat. The classic. The champion's protein.
Stockfish. The aroma of Sunday afternoon.
Ponmo in egusi. Chewy, delicious, traditional.

vegetables.onSelect: Efo shoko. The traditional leaf for traditional egusi.
Ugwu in egusi? Igbo influence. Delicious crossover.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil. The red that makes it right. Non-negotiable.

seasonings.onSelect: Onions. The foundation.
Fresh pepper. Heat that makes the swallow worthwhile.
Crayfish. Depth. Character. Soul.
Salt. Because even tradition needs seasoning.

teamA.name: Mortar & Pestle (By Hand)
teamA.tagline: The sweat is part of the flavor

teamB.name: Electric Yam Pounder
teamB.tagline: Smooth pounded yam, no gym required

recipes.name: Ogbono Soup (Draw Soup)
recipes.description: Slippery, viscous wild mango seed soup. The 'draw soup' that divides opinions.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Slip That Grips

bases_grains.onSelect: Ogbono seeds. Ground fine. The source of the legendary 'draw.'

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat ogbono. A classic combination.
Stockfish. The flavor anchor in the slippery sea.
Ponmo! Essential for the full ogbono experience.

vegetables.onSelect: Ugwu in ogbono. The Igbo way. Green, slightly bitter, perfect.
Bitter leaf ogbono? Bold. Your ancestors are intrigued.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil. The red that makes ogbono glow.

seasonings.onSelect: Onions. The base.
Pepper. Heat that cuts through the richness.
Crayfish. The flavor booster.
Ogiri! The fermented secret. Smells challenging, tastes divine.
Salt. The difference maker.

wildcards.onSelect: Ogbono AND okra? Double draw. Double trouble. The slip is REAL.

teamA.name: Grind My Own Ogbono
teamA.tagline: I trust no one with my draw

teamB.name: Pre-Ground Ogbono
teamB.tagline: The market woman already did the work

recipes.name: Pepper Soup
recipes.description: Spicy, aromatic, thin broth. The cure for cold nights, hangovers, and sadness.
recipes.alternateTitle: Liquid Fire. Emotional Support Soup.

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat pepper soup. The classic. The standard. The 'I've arrived at the party' order.
Catfish pepper soup. From the rivers to your bowl. Elegant.
Chicken pepper soup. Light. Comforting. The entry-level choice.

vegetables.onSelect: Onions. The aromatic base of the broth.
Fresh pepper. Lots of it. This is PEPPER soup, not 'mildly spiced water.'

seasonings.onSelect: Fresh ginger. The warmth. The kick. The 'I'm being healed' feeling.
Garlic. Because pepper soup without garlic is just sadness water.
Scent leaf (efirin). The authentic pepper soup herb. Aroma that travels.
Crayfish. Optional but recommended depth.
Seasoning cube. The shortcut. It works.
Salt. Season your healing.

wildcards.onSelect: Irish potatoes in pepper soup? Unorthodox. But it WORKS. Thickens the broth naturally.

recipes.name: Efo Riro (Vegetable Soup)
recipes.description: Rich, heavily-seasoned vegetable soup. The name literally means 'stirred vegetables.'
recipes.alternateTitle: When Vegetables Refuse to Be Boring

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat efo riro. The king of protein choices.
Stockfish. The aroma of serious cooking.
Ponmo. Chewy goodness.
Shaki! The texture king. Takes forever to cook but worth every minute.

vegetables.onSelect: Efo shoko. The traditional leaf. The reason 'riro' is in the name.
Tatashe. The red pepper that gives efo riro its signature look.
Scotch bonnet. Because efo riro should have attitude.
Onions. Generous amounts. The base of the base.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil. Abundant. Red. Glorious.

seasonings.onSelect: Iru! The fermented secret. Don't skip this. Your ancestors will frown.
Crayfish. Flavor reinforcement.
Maggi. Because we're not here to suffer.
Salt. Season every layer.

recipes.name: Chin Chin
recipes.description: Crunchy, sweet, fried dough cubes. The snack that disappears faster than you can make it.
recipes.alternateTitle: One Batch = Zero Leftovers. Every Time.

bases_grains.onSelect: All-purpose flour. The canvas for crunchy perfection.

proteins.onSelect: Eggs for richness. Optional but recommended.

oils_fats.onSelect: Butter. The richness that makes chin chin melt in your mouth.
Oil for deep frying. Hot and plentiful.

seasonings.onSelect: Sugar. The sweetness. The reason chin chin is a snack, not a cracker.
Grated nutmeg. The secret flavor that makes people ask 'what IS that?'
A pinch of salt. Balances the sweet.

wildcards.onSelect: Coconut chin chin? Tropical. Crunchy. Vacation in a bowl.

optional.name: Oven (Baked Chin Chin)
optional.onSelect: Baked chin chin? Healthier. Still crunchy. The gym crew approves.

recipes.name: Puff Puff (Fried Dough Balls)
recipes.description: Golden, fluffy, deep-fried dough balls. The people's snack. Party essential.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Ball That Unites Nigeria

bases_grains.onSelect: All-purpose flour. The foundation of fluffiness.

proteins.onSelect: Eggs in puff puff? Some do it. Richer, denser. Controversial in certain aunty circles.

oils_fats.onSelect: Oil for deep frying. Enough to let the puff puffs SWIM.

seasonings.onSelect: Sugar. Just enough to make them irresistible.
Nutmeg. The aroma that announces 'puff puff is coming.'
Pinch of salt. Balances the sweet. Essential.

seasonings.name: Yeast
seasonings.onSelect: Yeast. The leavening agent. The difference between puff puff and fried sadness.

wildcards.onSelect: Honey drizzle on puff puff? Sweet on sweet. You're dangerous.

recipes.name: Nigerian Fried Rice
recipes.description: Colorful, vegetable-studded fried rice. Jollof's lighter, more colorful sibling.
recipes.alternateTitle: Jollof's Colorful Cousin That Everyone Loves

bases_grains.onSelect: Long grain parboiled rice. Pre-cooked and cooled. No sticky rice allowed.

proteins.onSelect: Chicken fried rice. The classic pairing.
Prawn fried rice! Special occasion energy.

vegetables.onSelect: Diced carrots. The orange pop of color. Non-negotiable.
Green beans. The green crunch.
Green peas. Little green pearls of sweetness.
Onions. The aromatic base.
Mixed bell peppers. Color, crunch, sweetness.
Sweet corn. Golden bursts of sweetness.

oils_fats.onSelect: Vegetable oil for stir-frying. Hot wok, fast hands.
Butter finish. Richness that makes it restaurant-quality.

seasonings.onSelect: Maggi. Flavor shortcut. No shame.
Curry powder. The golden spice that defines Nigerian fried rice.
Thyme. Subtle herb note.
Garlic. Because flavor.
Ginger. Warmth and zing.
Salt. Season every layer.

optional.name: Large Pot (Parboil Rice)
optional.onSelect: Dedicated rice pot. Parboil perfectly, fry later.

recipes.name: Coconut Rice
recipes.description: Rice cooked in coconut milk. Rich, creamy, aromatic. Coastal elegance.
recipes.alternateTitle: When Rice Goes on a Beach Vacation

bases_grains.onSelect: Long grain rice. The vessel for coconut goodness.

proteins.onSelect: Prawns! The coastal protein. Perfect pairing.
Chicken coconut rice. Safe and satisfying.

vegetables.onSelect: Onions. The base.
Bell peppers. Color and sweetness.
Scotch bonnet. Gentle heat through the creaminess.

oils_fats.onSelect: Coconut milk! Fresh or canned. The STAR of this dish.
Coconut oil for toasting the rice. Double coconut. Double flavor.

seasonings.onSelect: Maggi. Savory balance to the sweetness.
Curry powder. Color and warmth.
Thyme. Subtle herb layer.
Salt. Balance the sweet and savory.

recipes.name: Asaro (Yam Porridge)
recipes.description: One-pot yam porridge. Comforting, filling, and surprisingly elegant.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Dish That Makes Yam the Main Character

bases_grains.onSelect: Fresh yam. Cut into chunks. The star of this one-pot wonder.

proteins.onSelect: Smoked fish. The smoky depth that defines asaro.
Dried fish. Umami punch.
Prawns in asaro? Elevated. Your guests will be impressed.

vegetables.onSelect: Onions. The base.
Fresh pepper. Blended. The heat that makes asaro wake up.
Tatashe. For color and sweetness.
Ugwu leaves added at the end. Green freshness.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil. Generous. The red that makes it asaro, not boiled yam.

seasonings.onSelect: Crayfish. Flavor depth.
Seasoning cube. One is enough.
Salt. Season the pot.

recipes.name: Nigerian Spaghetti (Spag)
recipes.description: Spaghetti cooked Nigerian-style — in a peppery, seasoned tomato stew. Student favorite.
recipes.alternateTitle: When Italian Meets Naija. And Naija Wins.

bases_grains.onSelect: Spaghetti. The pasta that surrendered to Nigerian seasoning.

proteins.onSelect: Corned beef spaghetti. The student special. Budget-friendly royalty.
Chicken spag. Elevated student meal.

vegetables.onSelect: Fresh tomatoes. Blended. The base of the stew.
Tatashe. Color and sweetness.
Scotch bonnet. Nigerian spag should have KICK.
Onions. Generous amounts.

oils_fats.onSelect: Vegetable oil for frying the stew base.

seasonings.onSelect: Tomato paste. Concentrated richness. Deep red color.
Maggi. The student's secret weapon.
Curry powder. Golden color. Nigerian spag must glow.
Thyme. Subtle herb.
Salt. Season the stew, season the pasta water.

wildcards.onSelect: Diced carrots in spag? Color. Crunch. Your aunty is side-eyeing but your mates approve.

optional.name: Colander (Drain Spag)
optional.onSelect: Proper draining. No soggy spag allowed.

recipes.name: Ogbono & Okra Soup (Combo Draw)
recipes.description: The ultimate draw soup combo. Ogbono AND okra together. Slippery luxury.
recipes.alternateTitle: Double Draw. Double Trouble. Zero Regrets.

bases_grains.onSelect: Ogbono. The primary draw. Ground fine.
Okra. The secondary draw. Sliced or grated. The combo is legendary.

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat. Classic.
Stockfish. The flavor anchor.
Ponmo. Essential chew.

vegetables.onSelect: Ugwu. The finishing green.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil. The red that makes it glow.

seasonings.onSelect: Onions. Base.
Pepper. Heat through the slip.
Crayfish. Depth.
Ogiri. The fermented secret weapon.
Salt.

grindingDebate.question: Team Grated Okra or Team Sliced Okra?

recipes.name: Banga Soup (Palm Nut Soup)
recipes.description: Rich, creamy soup extracted from palm nuts. Served with starch or swallow. Delta royalty.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Soup That Makes Palm Trees Proud

bases_grains.name: Palm Nut Extract
bases_grains.onSelect: Palm nut extract. The soul of banga. Extracted with effort and love.
bases_grains.note: Custom ingredient - different from regular palm oil

proteins.onSelect: Fresh catfish. The Delta classic. River-to-pot freshness.
Beef in banga. Hearty.

vegetables.onSelect: Onions. The base.
Scotch bonnet. Heat.

oils_fats.name: Extra Palm Oil (Optional)
oils_fats.onSelect: A little extra palm oil for richness. The Delta way.

seasonings.onSelect: Crayfish. Depth.
Ogiri. The fermented flavor base.
Maggi. Quick flavor.
Salt.
Scent leaf (efirin). The aromatic finish.

grindingDebate.question: Team Extract-Your-Own or Team Canned Banga?

recipes.name: Zobo (Hibiscus Drink)
recipes.description: Deep red, tangy-sweet hibiscus drink. Nigeria's unofficial national beverage.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Red Drink That Quenches Nations

vegetables.name: Dried Hibiscus Leaves (Zobo)
vegetables.onSelect: Dried hibiscus. The deep red soul of zobo. Without this, you're making... water.

seasonings.onSelect: Fresh ginger. Generous amounts. The kick that makes zobo ZOBO.
Garlic in zobo? Yes. The secret savory note. Trust the process.
Sugar. Balance the tang. Sweet but not cloying.
Honey instead of sugar? Healthier. Elegant. Your body thanks you.

wildcards.name: Pineapple Peel
wildcards.onSelect: Pineapple peel in your zobo brew? Tropical depth. Your guests will wonder what your secret is.

optional.name: Blender (Smooth Ginger/Garlic)
optional.onSelect: Blend the ginger and garlic for maximum flavor extraction.

recipes.name: Eba (Garri) & Egusi Soup
recipes.description: The everyday champion. Quick garri swallow with rich egusi. The people's choice.
recipes.alternateTitle: The Combo That Runs Nigeria

bases_grains.onSelect: Garri. Fine or coarse. The 5-minute swallow. Nigeria's true staple.
Egusi. Ground. The soup that makes eba feel like a feast.

proteins.onSelect: Goat meat. The classic.
Ponmo. Chewy satisfaction.

vegetables.onSelect: Efo shoko. The people's leaf.

oils_fats.onSelect: Palm oil. Red. Rich. Essential.

seasonings.onSelect: Onions.
Pepper.
Crayfish.
Salt.

grindingDebate.question: Team Hot Water Eba or Team Cooked Eba?

presetJudges.description: Pre-built judges that creators can use or customize.

verdicts.90-100: You cooked with the ancestors' blessing. I will give you my secret recipe.
verdicts.70-89: Good, but you took shortcuts. I saw that blender. Next time use the mortar.
verdicts.50-69: Is this what they teach you on YouTube? Come, let me show you properly.
verdicts.0-49: Who taught you to cook? Give me their phone number. I just want to talk.

verdicts.90-100: This is CONTENT! I'm posting this everywhere. #FoodieRevolution!
verdicts.70-89: Interesting twist! A bit safe, but your presentation is giving.
verdicts.50-69: Needs more fusion. Have you considered truffle oil? No? Okay...
verdicts.0-49: This is aggressively traditional. Where's the creativity? The pizzazz?

verdicts.90-100: Boss! This food is plenty AND fast? You're a lifesaver. Do you cater?
verdicts.70-89: Good portions. Could be faster, but I'm not complaining with a full stomach.
verdicts.50-69: Small portions, but the taste is there. I'll just eat more eba.
verdicts.0-49: I waited HOW long for this small thing? Omo, this is wickedness.

verdicts.90-100: Consistent, balanced, profitable. You're hired. When can you start?
verdicts.70-89: Good technique. With better time management, you could run a kitchen.
verdicts.50-69: This kitchen is chaos, but the food is edible. I've seen worse on opening night.
verdicts.0-49: My customers would demand a refund. And they'd be right.

loadingMessages: Somebody's grandmother is already judging your ingredient choices.
If you add unconventional ingredients, we won't stop you... but we will remember.
There are 371 tribes and countless food opinions. You're about to trigger several.
Palm oil detected. The ancestors have been notified.
Calibrating the 'Is this authentic?' detection algorithm...
Remember: Every dish has a story. Some stories are just more controversial.
Your cooking method is being pre-judged by aunties across the diaspora.
Warning: Screenshots of this meal may appear in family WhatsApp groups.
The secret ingredient is always confidence. And Maggi. Mostly Maggi.
Cooking time is directly proportional to how hungry your guests are.

finalVerdicts.75-100: Your dish will be remembered. Fondly. By everyone.
finalVerdicts.50-74: Not traditional. Not wrong. Just... conversation-starting.
finalVerdicts.25-49: The group chat has started arguing. Screenshots are circulating.
finalVerdicts.0-24: You cooked with confidence. Accuracy remains under active investigation.
